app_subtitle: Express unresolved feelings in a safe, private space

intro.title: The Unsent Letter
description: Sometimes, we carry deep feelings, hurts, or hopes that feel too difficult or scary to express directly to our partner.
Writing an unsent letter is a powerful therapeutic tool to organize your thoughts, release emotional weight, and find clarity without the fear of escalation.
This activity provides a completely private, unshared space to pour out your raw emotions honestly.
Remember: this letter is for your own healing and self-awareness, not for sending.
intro.notice: Write without filter. This is a secure space for your emotional release.

writing.title: Express What You've Been Holding In
writing.desc: Focus on your primary emotions (sadness, longing, hurt, fear) rather than blame. Speak directly to your partner, your ex-partner, or to your relationship itself.
prompts: What I find hardest to say is…
Deep down, the fear I carry is…
What hurt me most was when…
I wish you could truly understand…
What I need most from you is…
writing.placeholder:
Dear ________,
I want to tell you what I've been holding inside...
writing.notice: You can pause and come back anytime.

reflection.title: Honor Your Vulnerability
description: You've just put deep, perhaps painful, emotions into words. Take a slow, deep breath.
It is normal to feel a mix of sadness, relief, or exhaustion right now. Let yourself feel whatever is present.
Acknowledge the courage it took to be honest with yourself today.
reflection.notice: This was written for your own emotional clarity, not for partner confrontation.

history.title: Past Letters
history.empty_title: You haven't written any letters yet.
history.empty_desc: Your letters will appear here after you save them.

toasts.save_success: Letter saved to your private journal
toasts.auth_error: Auth session missing or DB not configured
